response.justification: Wei Chen is a capable backend engineer with 19 years of technical experience and genuine strength in distributed systems, microservices, and infrastructure. However, they fall critically short of CTO requirements: no evidence of leading and scaling teams to 30+, zero fintech domain expertise or regulated environment experience, and only one month of agentic AI exposure (insufficient for a "demonstrated production" requirement). This is a strong Staff/Principal Engineer candidate, not an executive CTO fit.
strengths: Deep distributed-systems expertise: high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby (millions of requests/second) and event-driven architecture with GISCollective
Full-stack backend capability: Node.js, AWS CDK, microservices design, DevOps infrastructure (Docker, CI/CD, GitLab), and database scaling
Proven adaptability: 19-year career across web, mobile, desktop, embedded systems, and multiple languages; current role includes AI-agent framework contributions
concerns: Zero demonstrated leadership at scale: no evidence of managing or growing teams; required experience is scaling from 10 to 30+ engineers in senior leadership roles

Agentic AI experience is unproven and recent (1 month at re:cinq starting April 2025); no evidence of designing production multi-agent systems, feedback loops, evaluation infrastructure, or observability for agent behavior

Missing fintech domain entirely: no credit-risk systems, payment flows, working-capital management, embedded finance, or regulated environment experience; most recent fintech role (RIDE Capital 2020–2021) lacks detail on actual domain depth
